weekplan.title: Weekly plan
weekplan.description: System baseline for the week based on training types, weekly goals and selected training days. Daily check-ins can still adjust today's final plan.

checkin.sleep_desc: Rate how good your sleep was last night.
checkin.energy_desc: Rate how much energy you feel right now.
checkin.soreness_desc: Rate how sore your body feels right now.
checkin.section.how_feel_copy: Choose the answer that fits best right now. It does not have to be perfect.
checkin.section.capacity_intro_copy: Choose the date and how much time today can realistically support.
checkin.section.context_copy: Optional. Use this only if it helps make today’s plan more precise.
checkin.notes_placeholder: E.g. slept well, legs a bit tired, 45 min available today
checkin.local_signals_intro: Optional. Mark only areas that need extra caution today.
review.rpe_help: Choose how hard the session felt.
review.rpe_help_long: RPE = how hard the session felt from 1 to 10. 1-3 very easy, 4-5 easy, 6-7 hard, 8-9 very hard, 10 max.

first_run.title: Welcome to SovereignStrength
first_run.intro: The app helps you choose today's training based on check-in, equipment, and your preferences.
first_run.flow: Flow: overview → check-in → today's plan → after training.
first_run.setup_reason: The better your profile and equipment are set up, the more useful your first recommendation becomes.

checklist.profile_help: Add height and bodyweight for more useful recommendations.
checklist.training_types_help: Choose whether you want running, strength, bodyweight, and/or mobility.
checklist.equipment_help: Mark which equipment you actually have access to.
checklist.planning_help: Choose training days so the app can shape the first plan more intelligently.
checklist.physiology_help: Menstruation-related considerations are optional and can be configured later.

first_run.next_step_ready: You can now continue to your first check-in.

onboarding.first_run.title: Welcome to SovereignStrength
onboarding.first_run.intro: This is your calm first overview of how the app works.
onboarding.first_run.flow: The flow is simple: setup, check-in, today's plan, and after training.
onboarding.first_run.setup_reason: Fill in your profile, choose training types, and add the equipment you have access to. Then the app can create a first plan that fits you.
onboarding.first_run.checklist.profile_h: Add height and bodyweight for more useful recommendations.
onboarding.first_run.checklist.training_: Choose whether you want running, strength, bodyweight, and/or mobility.
onboarding.first_run.checklist.equipment: Mark which equipment you actually have access to.
onboarding.first_run.checklist.planning_: Choose training days so the app can shape the first plan more intelligently.
onboarding.first_run.checklist.physiolog: Menstruation-related considerations are optional and can be configured later.
onboarding.first_run.next_step_ready: You can now continue to your first check-in.
cardio.target.base_talk_variable: {minutes} min easy run at conversational pace
cardio.target.easy_walk_or_jog_variable: {minutes} min easy walk or very light jog
onboarding.first_run.finish_default: Review your setup and save when you are ready to continue to your first check-in.
onboarding.first_run.finish_ready: Your minimum setup is ready. Save and continue to your first check-in.
onboarding.first_run.finish_missing: You still need {count} core setup area(s) before the first recommendation is ready.
onboarding.first_run.section.basic_profi: Add the minimum body data needed for a better first recommendation.
onboarding.first_run.section.training_ty: Choose the kinds of training you want the app to plan for.
onboarding.first_run.section.planning_bo: Pick training days and a rough weekly target so the first plan is less generic.
onboarding.first_run.section.equipment_b: Tell the app what you actually have available, plus the load increments it should use.
onboarding.first_run.section.physiology_: Optional. Only turn this on if you want menstruation-related check-in considerations included.
Show optional menstruation fields in check-in
plan.rest_day_default_summary: Rest is the default today. Light movement or manual training is optional.
profile.recommended_strength_program_def: This program is a better fit for your current weekly target and setup.
today_plan.return_to_autoplan_confirm: Remove the manual override and return to autoplan for today?
profile.strength_starting_profile_help: Choose how gently or ambitiously the strength side should start. This affects your first program choice.
profile.strength_starting_profile_help_l: Conservative / re-entry = calm restart or return after a break · Beginner = new or only recently training · Novice = more habit and somewhat higher load tolerance · Intermediate = solid training habit and need for more demanding program matching.
profile.run_starting_profile_help: Choose how gently or ambitiously the running side should start. This affects your first program choice.
profile.run_starting_profile_help_levels: Conservative / re-entry = start gently or return after a break · Beginner = new or only a little recent running · Novice = more habit and slightly higher capacity.
onboarding.first_run.section.planning_le: Your starting level helps the app choose how conservative or ambitious the first program recommendation should be.
onboarding.first_run.finish_selection_hi: Your first program is chosen from your training types, weekly target, equipment, and starting level.
profile.program_why_equipment_barbell_be: your equipment matches barbell and bench
profile.program_why_equipment_basic: your equipment matches the selected level
profile.program_why_strength: The strength program was chosen from {week_goal}, {equipment}, and {starting_level}.
profile.program_why_run: The running program was chosen from {week_goal} and {starting_level}.
forecast.missing_training_types_summary: Choose at least one training type to get your first plan.
forecast.missing_training_types_reason: The app can only make a real recommendation once you have chosen strength, running, or both.
today_plan.missing_training_types_lead: Choose at least one training type in profile and equipment before the app can build your first plan.
profile.recommended_current_strength_pro: No active strength program is selected yet
review.closure_intro: Briefly review how the session went. This helps the next recommendation fit your training better.
review.finish_lead: Take a moment to log how the session went. This helps the next recommendation fit your training better.
review.saved_summary.cardio_saved: Today's cardio session has been saved.
review.saved_summary.cardio_recovery_kee: Keep the next recovery run easy and unforced.
review.saved_summary.cardio_recovery_tak: Take the next run easier so recovery work stays restorative.
review.saved_summary.cardio_base_repeat_: Repeat a similar controlled effort next time.
review.saved_summary.cardio_base_easier: Keep the next base run slightly easier so it stays aerobic.
review.saved_summary.cardio_base_take_ea: Take the next base session easier and keep the pace under control.
review.saved_summary.cardio_tempo_more_c: The next tempo session can be a little more committed if recovery is good.
review.saved_summary.cardio_tempo_contro: Recover well, then keep the next tempo session controlled but purposeful.
review.saved_summary.cardio_tempo_easier: Take the next quality run slightly easier so tempo work stays repeatable.
review.saved_summary.cardio_intervals_re: Recover before the next hard run and keep easy days easy.
review.saved_summary.cardio_intervals_cl: Make the next interval session more clearly structured or slightly harder if appropriate.
review.saved_summary.cardio_log_more_dat: Log duration, distance, and effort next time for a more useful running review.
review.saved_summary.cardio_log_intent: Log the cardio type and effort more clearly next time.
review.saved_summary.cardio_recovery_mat: Recovery run matched the intended easy effort.
review.saved_summary.cardio_recovery_too: Recovery run was harder than intended.
review.saved_summary.cardio_base_matched: Base run broadly matched the intended steady effort.
review.saved_summary.cardio_base_slightl: Base run drifted a bit harder than intended.
review.saved_summary.cardio_base_too_har: Base run was too hard for its intended purpose.
review.saved_summary.cardio_tempo_matche: Tempo run broadly matched the intended sustained hard effort.
review.saved_summary.cardio_tempo_too_ea: Tempo run looked easier than intended.
review.saved_summary.cardio_tempo_too_ha: Tempo run was harder than intended.
review.saved_summary.cardio_intervals_ma: Interval session broadly matched the intended hard structured effort.
review.saved_summary.cardio_intervals_to: Interval session looked easier than intended.
review.saved_summary.cardio_not_enough_d: Cardio session saved, but there was not enough data to assess the run.
Not enough cardio data was available for a running-specific review.
review.saved_summary.cardio_recovery_det: Effort stayed low enough for recovery work.
review.saved_summary.cardio_base_detail_: The effort looks a little high for base work.
RPE {value} fits a controlled base run.
review.saved_summary.cardio_tempo_detail: The effort looks closer to base than tempo work.
review.saved_summary.cardio_intervals_de: The effort does not clearly stand out from an easier steady run.
review.saved_summary.progress_next_time: You can probably progress next time.
profile.training_goal_help: Used to choose more relevant programs when the catalog supports the goal.
profile.starter_capacity_profile_help: Choose how light or robust your overall starting capacity is right now. This is used as an early planning signal.
profile.starter_capacity_profile_help_le: Very low capacity = very gentle start with a low threshold · Low capacity = cautious start with simple loading · General beginner = normal beginner starting point · Loaded beginner = new, but ready for a more direct start.
strength_journey.reentry: A gentle return-to-strength path with lower friction and a focus on getting started consistently.
strength_journey.minimal_dose: A short and simple strength path that prioritizes adherence and low friction.
strength_journey.beginner_home: A beginner-friendly full-body strength path for home training with simple structure and clear main movements.
strength_journey.beginner_gym: A beginner-friendly gym strength path built around a simple base of squat, press, and pull.
strength_journey.base_home: A home-based base strength path with more progression and slightly more total training work.
strength_journey.base_gym: A base gym strength path focused on stable main lifts and clear progression.
strength_journey.upper_lower: A more structured upper/lower strength path with higher frequency and a clearer weekly rhythm.
strength_journey.hybrid_support: A strength path meant to support a hybrid setup without taking all the focus away from running or mixed training.
running_journey.reentry: A gentle return-to-running path with lower load and room to build consistency again.
running_journey.beginner_start: A beginner-friendly running start with manageable frequency and a clear path into regular running.
running_journey.base_5k_10k: A running base with enough structure to point toward 5K and 10K without becoming rigid or overcomplicated.
running_journey.base_10k_half: A more extended running base with room for longer sessions and progression toward 10K or half marathon base work.
running_journey.hybrid: A hybrid running path where running and strength are meant to work together instead of competing for all the space.

manual_template.save_requires_entries: Add at least one exercise before saving a template.
custom_workout.title: Saved custom workouts
custom_workout.save_requires_entries: Add at least one exercise before saving a workout.
history.weekly_rhythm_none_completed: No completed sessions yet this week
profile.local_protection_holds_help: Keep a region in caution or protect state if it should still be protected across multiple planning days.
today_plan.local_adjustment_scope_help: Adjusts only this exercise today. Program switches and loaded alternatives are handled through program recommendations.
plan.weekplan_adjusted_to_today: The weekly plan suggested {planned}, but today's plan was adjusted to {actual}.
today_plan.local_protection_adjusted: Today's plan was adjusted to protect {regions}.
weekplan.weekly_goal_complete_guidance: Weekly goal reached. Recover until your next planned training day.
